audit_items.proof_trace: All public LMDB API entry points validate NULL pointers before dereference: mdb_put (L9033) checks !key||!data, mdb_txn_begin (L2857) checks calloc result, mdb_txn_commit (L3451) checks txn==NULL, and all allocation results (mdb_midl_alloc, malloc, calloc) are checked before use in critical paths. Internal static functions rely on validated inputs from callers within the same file. mdb_cursor_put (L6563) omits a data!=NULL guard but every caller on the LDAP-reachable path (mdb_put at L9045, and all internal call sites at L3179/3228/3283/3638/7007/7035/9836) passes non-NULL MDB_val pointers, making a NULL dereference at L6588 unreachable in practice.
